evals.prompt: Evaluate the meaningfulness of the unit tests in this project. It is a plain pytest project; run the tests with `python -m pytest`. Ignore EXPECTED.md — it is not part of the project under evaluation.
evals.expected_output: mutation-report.md classifying each of the 8 tests per the fixture's engineered ground truth (test_add OK, test_clamp_upper OK, test_discount_vacuous SUSPECT, test_format_price and test_format_price_dup COUPLED with each other, test_normalize_identity BASELINE explicitly marked as the root of its group, test_normalize_strips and test_normalize_lowercases OK and each explicitly marked as a sibling of test_normalize_identity), plus untested-areas.md listing safe_div. The fixture sources are left unmodified and the suite is green at the end.
expectations: mutation-report.md exists and contains exactly one row per test in the fixture suite (8 tests: test_add, test_clamp_upper, test_discount_vacuous, test_format_price, test_format_price_dup, test_normalize_identity, test_normalize_strips, test_normalize_lowercases)
test_add is classified OK with a non-empty mutation patch that modifies calc/core.py
test_format_price and test_format_price_dup are classified COUPLED, each listing the other as its sibling
test_normalize_identity is classified BASELINE and its report row explicitly marks it as the root of its baseline group
test_normalize_strips is classified OK and its report row explicitly marks it as a sibling of test_normalize_identity (the concrete root is identifiable by test name or number)
test_normalize_lowercases is classified OK and its report row explicitly marks it as a sibling of test_normalize_identity (the concrete root is identifiable by test name or number)
untested-areas.md (or the report's coverage-gap section) mentions safe_div
after the run, all files under calc/ and tests/ are byte-identical to the original fixture (every mutation was reverted)
the full test suite (8 tests) passes at the end of the run
